summary: Joel Spolsky announces the Russian translation of his book 'Joel on Software.' The post is extremely brief, consisting of just two sentences: one announcing the book's availability and one humorously noting he doesn't recognize the people depicted on the cover. It's a lighthearted promotional post with no substantive technical content.
key_points: The Russian translation of 'Joel on Software' was published at a price of 250 rubles
Joel humorously distances himself from the cover design choices, noting he doesn't know who the people on the cover are
The post reflects the international reach of Joel's writing and brand
money_quotes: I have no idea who those people are on the cover.
key_insight: A brief announcement post about the Russian translation of Joel's book, notable mainly for its dry humor about the cover art.
